chief_complaint: A 49-year-old male patient presented with an acute coronary syndrome without ST elevation.
history: The patient had no medical history. He was incidentally diagnosed with two congenital complex coronary artery abnormalities (CAA) during the management of his acute coronary syndrome.
exam: No specific physical examination findings were reported.
labs: The coronary angiogram showed an acute atheromatous occlusion of the right coronary artery (RCA) treated by a drug-eluting stent. Transcatheter angiograms and coronary computed tomography (CT) revealed an ectopic RCA arising from the left cuspid with an inter-arterial course, and a second left anterior descending artery emerging from the RCA with a retro-pulmonary course.
correct_diagnosis: Acute coronary syndrome due to atheromatous disease
differentials: Acute coronary syndrome due to atheromatous disease
Myocardial infarction due to congenital coronary artery anomalies
correct_ranking: Acute coronary syndrome due to atheromatous disease
Myocardial infarction due to congenital coronary artery anomalies
